themes.reasoning_notes: Repeatable detain on attack locks down blockers, attackers, and activated abilities, fitting Azorius board-control play patterns.
Detaining up to two permanents each attack creates strong swing turns and helps a flying 4/5 close the game.
Attack-triggered detain is temporary board suppression, creating swing turns rather than permanent control; flying body helps convert that into pressure.
Repeatable detain can support control shells, but its attack-gated, until-next-turn effect is more tempo-oriented than classic control.
